content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reject the `br list` hard-fail amendment because fail-closed canonical prompt hydration is intentional and still bypassable with a prompt override; accept the nearby-detail failure and unbounded fanout amendments because they expose real correctness and robustness defects.

## Votes

- `fr-13-6ada3d44`: Reject
  The `load_bead_summaries(...)?` change is a deliberate fail-closed behavior for canonical prompt generation, not a defect on its own. This command now relies on `br list` for authoritative dependency/dependent, planned-elsewhere, and nearby-work context; falling back to empty summaries would knowingly emit unknown or stale status/title data. There is also still an explicit escape hatch via `--prompt-file`, so this does not make project creation impossible in general. Restoring the old degraded path would trade a clear failure for silently lower-integrity prompts.
- `fr-13-64fbce41`: Accept
  `load_nearby_details_for_ids` currently turns every `load_bead_detail` failure into `None`, and the render path then falls back to summary/relation data as though the refresh succeeded. That is unsafe because nearby rendering intentionally uses detail to override stale summary status/title/description and to suppress closed or deferred items. If a `br show` fails, the user can be shown stale nearby work with no indication that the authoritative refresh failed.
- `fr-13-7bb1dc84`: Accept
  The production path is unbounded. `nearby_all_direct_relation_detail_candidate_ids` returns every direct dependency/dependent, and `load_nearby_details_for_ids` launches one concurrent `br show` per id through `FuturesUnordered` with no runtime semaphore or cap before any render budget is applied. The only direct-detail cap is test-only. Highly connected beads can therefore trigger large subprocess fanout during project creation, which is a real robustness and resource-control bug.
